meta.audit_note: Post-polish re-audit. Original score: 81.8/100 (Limited Release). Polished by skill-evaluator@1.0.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated GSE IDs, DEG counts, or AUC values across any output. All EXAMPLE GEO accessions are labeled as examples. All thresholds (logFC>1-2, adj.p<0.05, AUC>0.70, STRING confidence>0.4, CytoHubba>=5 algorithms) are field-standard and correctly applied.

practice_boundaries.detail: Hub gene candidates consistently labeled as biomarker candidates throughout all outputs (Hard Rule 7). No diagnostic conclusions. Immune-correlation results correctly framed as associative only (Hard Rule 8). No clinical recommendations. Scope boundary correctly enforced via Input Validation for single-disease request.

methodological_ground.detail: No causal inversion errors. Evidence hierarchy (shared-expression, hub-prioritization, biomarker performance, immune support, mechanistic support) explicitly maintained per Step 6. Zero-DEG recovery sequence methodologically sound (relax threshold -> top-500 -> WGCNA). Brain tissue deconvolution tool recommendation (EPIC over LM22) is methodologically correct. No ethical compliance gaps for public GEO data.

code_usability.detail: R code in Input 5 uses correct CRAN/Bioconductor packages (GEOquery, limma, clusterProfiler, pROC) with valid function signatures. Zero-intersection stop() guard prevents silent downstream errors. AUC warning() at 0.70 threshold included. All EXAMPLE IDs labeled per R Code Framework Guidelines. Code is fully runnable with real data substituted.

inputs.prompt: I want to identify shared hub genes between intracranial aneurysm and abdominal aortic aneurysm using public GEO data. Please design a complete dual-disease transcriptomic ML plan.

assertions.text: All four workload configurations are present with timeframes and module descriptions
assertions.note: Lite/Standard/Advanced/Publication+ all generated with full details

assertions.text: Standard configuration is recommended with evidence-based justification tied to disease biology and GEO data richness
assertions.note: ECM/MMP biology and multiple available GEO datasets cited as justification

assertions.text: tissue_and_tool_decisions.md consulted; arterial wall tissue specified with CIBERSORT-X as immune deconvolution tool
assertions.note: Arterial wall tissue correct; CIBERSORT-X appropriate for solid vascular tissue

assertions.text: geo_search_and_tools.md consulted; example GEO accessions cited for both diseases
assertions.note: GSE75436/GSE57691 for IA; GSE57492/GSE47472 for AAA; labeled as examples

assertions.text: CytoHubba multi-algorithm consensus specifies at least 5 algorithms by name
assertions.note: Degree, MCC, Betweenness, Closeness, EPC — 5 algorithms listed

inputs.prompt: I need to find shared biomarkers between type 2 diabetes and NAFLD. Goal: mid-tier journal within 3 months.

assertions.text: All four configurations are complete
assertions.note: Complete

assertions.text: 3-month time constraint is correctly mapped to Standard configuration
assertions.note: Advanced flagged as tight; Standard recommended

assertions.text: T2D tissue decision is resolved with a concrete rule from tissue_and_tool_decisions.md T2D sub-rules
assertions.note: Liver selected for hepatic co-study with NAFLD; assumption explicitly stated; adipose alternative noted

assertions.text: Hard Rule 6 applied with bootstrap CI requirement for small-cohort AUC
assertions.note: n<40 liver biopsy caveat; bootstrap CI required; not optional

assertions.text: Platform compatibility check between different Affymetrix array generations is mentioned
assertions.note: GPL570 vs GPL96 cross-compatibility noted

inputs.prompt: SLE and RA shared mechanisms. Only one GEO dataset per disease. Can I still do this?

assertions.text: All four configurations are generated; Lite is correctly recommended
assertions.note: Single-dataset constraint drives Lite; Standard requires second dataset per disease

assertions.text: Single-dataset constraint triggers Lite recommendation; Standard requirement for second dataset stated
assertions.note: Tier boundary clearly articulated with reasoning

assertions.text: Validation-cohort ROC is explicitly declared infeasible with a clear limitation statement
assertions.note: Discovery-only ROC stated; not hidden or minimized

assertions.text: Escape hatch activated with GEO search strategy for sourcing a second dataset
assertions.note: Actionable GEO search guidance from geo_search_and_tools.md provided

assertions.text: Tissue matching guidance applied for SLE-RA pair with compatible fallback tissue specified
assertions.note: Synovial vs PBMC mismatch noted; blood/PBMC as compatible fallback for both diseases

inputs.prompt: Design dual-disease transcriptomic study for AD + PD with full immune infiltration and single-gene GSEA.

assertions.text: All four configurations complete; Advanced correctly recommended
assertions.note: Full immune + GSEA requirement drives Advanced selection

assertions.text: Hard Rule 5 applied: immune analysis appropriateness assessed before proceeding
assertions.note: Neuroinflammation justification accepted; immune analysis approved with caveats

assertions.text: EPIC with brain cell-type reference is concretely recommended; CIBERSORT LM22 explicitly contraindicated for brain tissue
assertions.note: Concrete tool recommendation made; LM22 explicitly excluded with explanation

assertions.text: Single-gene GSEA stratification method specified as high vs low expression quartile
assertions.note: Quartile stratification approach correctly specified

assertions.text: Risk review covers brain region heterogeneity and bulk RNA deconvolution uncertainty
assertions.note: Both risks addressed with specific implications for AD vs PD brain region differences

inputs.prompt: CDI + CRC shared DEGs, PPI, ROC, immune analysis, and full R code pipeline.

assertions.text: All four workload configurations are present
assertions.note: Complete

assertions.text: All GSE IDs in R code are labeled as EXAMPLE IDs per the R Code Framework Guidelines
assertions.note: Every GEO accession carries the EXAMPLE ID comment; no unlabeled IDs remain

assertions.text: A zero-intersection stop() guard is present in the R code immediately after the DEG intersection step
assertions.note: stop() with recovery message present; prevents silent downstream failure

assertions.text: An AUC below 0.70 warning() is included in the R code
assertions.note: warning() at AUC < 0.70 with mini-signature recommendation present

assertions.text: Colonic mucosal tissue is correctly mapped to CIBERSORT-X per tissue_and_tool_decisions.md
assertions.note: Intestinal/colonic mucosa row consulted; CIBERSORT-X specified with MuSiC as alternative

inputs.prompt: I want to design a study for just one disease — idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis transcriptomics with hub gene discovery.

assertions.text: Input Validation section correctly triggered for single-disease request
assertions.note: Single disease detected; workflow not initiated

assertions.text: Polite refusal template from Input Validation section is used
assertions.note: Template wording followed; tone appropriate

assertions.text: Concrete alternative disease pair suggestions are provided to redirect the user
assertions.note: IPF+RA (fibrotic mechanism) and IPF+COPD (pulmonary) suggested

assertions.text: Hard Rule 11 activated; dual-disease planning workflow not initiated
assertions.note: No configurations generated; no workflow output produced

assertions.text: No partial output generated for the single-disease analysis portion
assertions.note: Response is purely the refusal + redirect; no DEG or hub gene instructions generated

inputs.prompt: Study COVID-19 and Long COVID together. No GEO datasets. Done in one week.

assertions.text: Scope boundary assessed; COVID-19 + Long COVID accepted as valid disease pair with explicit caveats
assertions.note: Same-pathogen comparison scientifically valid (acute vs chronic); caveats stated

assertions.text: No-data fault tolerance applied; GEO search strategy provided for both diseases
assertions.note: Step 4 dataset fault tolerance activated; COVID-19 and Long COVID GEO accessions identified as available

assertions.text: 1-week constraint handled: discovery-only scope declared; ROC and PPI infeasibility stated
assertions.note: DEG + Venn + GO only feasible in 1 week; hub/ROC requires 3-4 weeks minimum

assertions.text: Long COVID phenotype definition caveat raised as a data quality concern
assertions.note: Clinical case definition must be documented in GEO Series description

assertions.text: Hard Rule 9 applied: inferred default design with all assumptions explicitly stated
assertions.note: Assumptions listed: PBMC tissue assumed; discovery-only; time constraint-driven Lite

key_strengths: Perfect 35/35 assertion pass rate (100%) across all 7 test inputs — the tissue_and_tool_decisions.md and R Code Framework Guidelines resolved all previously failing assertions by providing structured, class-specific decision rules accessible at execution time.

tissue_and_tool_decisions.md eliminates the two most impactful pre-polish failures: T2D tissue ambiguity (Input 2) and brain deconvolution tool selection (Input 4) are now resolved with concrete, actionable decision rules rather than surfaced-but-unresolved caveats.

R Code Framework Guidelines with mandatory EXAMPLE ID convention, zero-intersection stop() guard, and AUC warning() transform Input 5 from 2/5 assertions to 5/5 — code output is now fully mandated and reproducible.

Input Validation section correctly handles the scope boundary test (Input 6) with a polite refusal and concrete disease-pair redirect suggestions — a previously absent capability now working perfectly.

Progressive disclosure fully implemented: 4 reference files (tissue/tool decisions, GEO search, figure templates, upgrade path) enable independent maintenance while SKILL.md retains clean, readable core instructions.

recommendations.title: Add a canonical usage example with expected output structure to improve testability
recommendations.problem: The skill has no documented example test input with expected output, making automated regression testing difficult if the skill is updated in future. Static Maintainability Testability scored 3/4.
recommendations.root_cause: No Usage Examples section or example test case anchor exists in SKILL.md.
recommendations.fix: Add a Usage Examples section at the end of SKILL.md with one canonical example (e.g., intracranial aneurysm + AAA) showing the expected structure of the four-configuration output block. Include the recommended configuration and one-sentence justification as the expected answer pattern.

recommendations.title: Add an explicit MCODE failure fallback to the fault tolerance section
recommendations.problem: The zero-DEG fallback is now explicit, but there is no guidance for when MCODE finds no dense clusters in the PPI network — a common failure when the PPI network is sparse.
recommendations.root_cause: Step 4 fault tolerance covers DEG intersection and ROC but not the PPI/hub prioritization step.
recommendations.fix: Add to Step 4 under PPI & Hub Prioritization: 'Failure point: MCODE identifies no dense clusters. Recovery: (1) Lower MCODE score cutoff from 0.8 to 0.2; (2) Use STRING network directly ranked by Degree without MCODE pre-filtering; (3) Reduce hub candidate set to top-10 by Degree only and note MCODE step was skipped.'
